definition: To stop working or functioning; to lose control of emotions
exampleSentence: My car broke down on the highway during rush hour.
exampleTranslation: 我的车在高峰期时在高速公路上抛锚了。

definition: To enter a place illegally by force; to start doing something suddenly
exampleSentence: Someone broke into our office and stole two laptops.
exampleTranslation: 有人闯入我们的办公室偷了两台笔记本电脑。

definition: To start suddenly, especially something unpleasant like war or disease
exampleSentence: A fire broke out in the factory late last night.
exampleTranslation: 昨晚深夜工厂发生了火灾。

definition: To end a romantic relationship; to separate into smaller parts
exampleSentence: They broke up after dating for three years.
exampleTranslation: 他们交往三年后分手了。

definition: To cause something to happen
exampleSentence: The new policy brought about significant changes in the workplace.
exampleTranslation: 新政策给工作场所带来了重大变化。

definition: To return something; to cause memories or feelings to return
exampleSentence: This song always brings back memories of my childhood.
exampleTranslation: 这首歌总是让我回忆起童年。

definition: To mention a topic; to raise a child
exampleSentence: She was brought up by her grandparents in a small village.
exampleTranslation: 她由祖父母在一个小村庄抚养长大。

definition: To return a phone call to someone
exampleSentence: I missed her call, so I need to call her back.
exampleTranslation: 我没接到她的电话，所以需要回电话给她。

definition: To cancel a planned event or activity
exampleSentence: They called off the outdoor concert because of the heavy rain.
exampleTranslation: 由于大雨，他们取消了户外音乐会。

definition: To continue doing something
exampleSentence: Despite the difficulties, she carried on with her research.
exampleTranslation: 尽管困难重重，她还是继续她的研究。

definition: To complete or perform a task, plan, or order
exampleSentence: The team carried out a series of experiments to test the theory.
exampleTranslation: 团队进行了一系列实验来验证这个理论。

definition: To reach the same level as someone; to talk with someone after a long time
exampleSentence: Let's meet for coffee this weekend and catch up.
exampleTranslation: 我们这周末见面喝咖啡叙叙旧吧。

definition: To register at a hotel or airport
definitionZh: 登记入住；办理登机手续
exampleSentence: We need to check in at the hotel before 3 PM.
exampleTranslation: 我们需要在下午三点前办理酒店入住。

definition: To leave a hotel; to examine or look at something
exampleSentence: Check out this new app — it's really useful for learning languages.
exampleTranslation: 看看这个新应用——学语言真的很有用。

definition: To find or meet by chance; to give a particular impression
exampleSentence: I came across an interesting article about space travel.
exampleTranslation: 我偶然看到一篇关于太空旅行的有趣文章。

definition: To be mentioned or arise in conversation; to approach
exampleSentence: The topic of salary came up during the meeting.
exampleTranslation: 会议中提到了薪资的话题。

definition: To think of or produce an idea or plan
exampleSentence: She came up with a brilliant solution to the problem.
exampleTranslation: 她想出了一个解决问题的绝妙方案。

definition: To reduce the amount or quantity of something
exampleSentence: The doctor advised him to cut down on sugar and salt.
exampleTranslation: 医生建议他减少糖和盐的摄入。

definition: To disconnect or stop the supply of something; to interrupt someone
exampleSentence: The storm cut off electricity to thousands of homes.
exampleTranslation: 暴风雨导致数千户家庭断电。

definition: To stop doing something; to remove by cutting
exampleSentence: You should cut out junk food if you want to lose weight.
exampleTranslation: 如果你想减肥，就应该戒掉垃圾食品。

definition: To handle or take action regarding a problem or situation
exampleSentence: She's very good at dealing with difficult customers.
exampleTranslation: 她非常擅长应对难缠的顾客。

definition: To take someone or something to a place and leave them there
exampleSentence: Can you drop me off at the train station on your way to work?
exampleTranslation: 你上班路上能顺便送我到火车站吗？

definition: To leave school or a course before finishing; to withdraw
exampleSentence: He dropped out of college to start his own business.
exampleTranslation: 他从大学退学去创业了。

definition: To eventually reach a place or situation, often unexpectedly
exampleSentence: We got lost and ended up in a completely different town.
exampleTranslation: 我们迷路了，最后到了一个完全不同的城镇。

definition: To break into pieces; to fail completely
exampleSentence: The old book was falling apart, so I handled it carefully.
exampleTranslation: 这本旧书快要散架了，所以我小心翼翼地拿着。

definition: To fail to keep up with others in progress or development
exampleSentence: If you miss too many classes, you'll fall behind.
exampleTranslation: 如果你缺课太多，就会落后。

definition: To understand or solve something after thinking about it
exampleSentence: It took me a while to figure out how to use the new software.
exampleTranslation: 我花了一段时间才弄明白如何使用这个新软件。

definition: To complete a form or document by writing information
exampleSentence: Please fill in the application form with your personal details.
exampleTranslation: 请在申请表上填写你的个人信息。

definition: To complete a form or questionnaire
exampleSentence: You need to fill out this form before seeing the doctor.
exampleTranslation: 看医生之前你需要填写这张表格。

definition: To discover or learn something new
exampleSentence: I just found out that the meeting has been postponed.
exampleTranslation: 我刚发现会议已经推迟了。

definition: To have a friendly relationship with someone
exampleSentence: She gets along well with all of her coworkers.
exampleTranslation: 她和所有同事相处得很好。

definition: To escape or leave a place; to go on vacation
exampleSentence: We need to get away for a few days and relax.
exampleTranslation: 我们需要出去度几天假放松一下。

definition: To manage to survive or live with limited resources
exampleSentence: It's hard to get by on such a low salary in this city.
exampleTranslation: 在这个城市靠这么低的工资很难维持生活。

definition: To recover from an illness or emotional experience
exampleSentence: It took her months to get over the breakup.
exampleTranslation: 她花了好几个月才从分手中恢复过来。

definition: To rise from bed or from a sitting position
exampleSentence: I usually get up at 6:30 on weekdays.
exampleTranslation: 我工作日通常六点半起床。

definition: To give something free of charge; to reveal a secret
exampleSentence: She gave away all her old clothes to charity.
exampleTranslation: 她把所有旧衣服都捐给了慈善机构。

definition: To stop trying; to quit a habit
exampleSentence: Don't give up — you're so close to reaching your goal.
exampleTranslation: 别放弃——你离目标已经很近了。

definition: To proceed or continue with something
exampleSentence: If everyone agrees, let's go ahead with the plan.
exampleTranslation: 如果大家都同意，我们就继续执行这个计划。

definition: To continue; to happen
exampleSentence: What's going on? I heard a lot of noise outside.
exampleTranslation: 怎么了？我听到外面很多噪音。

definition: To experience something difficult; to examine carefully
exampleSentence: She's going through a really tough time at work right now.
exampleTranslation: 她现在工作上正经历一段非常艰难的时期。

definition: To develop from a child into an adult
exampleSentence: He grew up in a small town in the countryside.
exampleTranslation: 他在乡下的一个小镇长大。

definition: To submit something, such as homework or a report
exampleSentence: Please hand in your assignments by the end of the week.
exampleTranslation: 请在本周末之前提交你的作业。

definition: To distribute something to a group of people
exampleSentence: The teacher handed out the test papers to the students.
exampleTranslation: 老师把试卷分发给学生。

definition: To give something to someone, often reluctantly or formally
exampleSentence: The thief was forced to hand over the stolen goods to the police.
exampleTranslation: 小偷被迫把赃物交给了警察。

definition: To spend time relaxing or socializing informally
exampleSentence: We usually hang out at the park after school.
exampleTranslation: 我们放学后通常在公园闲逛。

definition: To end a phone call; to put something on a hook or hanger
exampleSentence: She hung up the phone before I could finish speaking.
exampleTranslation: 我还没说完她就挂了电话。

definition: To wait for a short time; to grip tightly
exampleSentence: Hold on a moment — I'll be right back.
exampleTranslation: 等一下——我马上回来。

definition: To delay; to rob using threats or weapons
exampleSentence: Sorry I'm late — I was held up in traffic.
exampleTranslation: 抱歉我迟到了——我被堵在路上了。

definition: To continue doing something without stopping
exampleSentence: Keep on practicing and you'll get better at playing the guitar.
exampleTranslation: 继续练习，你的吉他会越弹越好的。

definition: To maintain the same speed or level as others
exampleSentence: She walks so fast that I can barely keep up with her.
exampleTranslation: 她走得太快了，我几乎跟不上她。

definition: To dismiss workers because there is not enough work; to stop doing something
exampleSentence: The company laid off 200 employees due to budget cuts.
exampleTranslation: 公司因为预算削减解雇了200名员工。

definition: To not include something or someone; to omit
exampleSentence: Make sure you don't leave out any important details in the report.
exampleTranslation: 确保报告中不要遗漏任何重要细节。

definition: To disappoint someone by failing to do what was expected
exampleSentence: I trusted him, but he really let me down this time.
exampleTranslation: 我信任他，但这次他真的让我失望了。

definition: To take care of someone or something
exampleSentence: Could you look after my cat while I'm on vacation?
exampleTranslation: 我度假的时候你能帮我照顾猫吗？

definition: To try to find something or someone
exampleSentence: I've been looking for my keys for the past twenty minutes.
exampleTranslation: 我找钥匙已经找了二十分钟了。

definition: To feel excited and happy about something that is going to happen
exampleSentence: I'm really looking forward to the summer holiday.
exampleTranslation: 我非常期待暑假。

definition: To investigate or examine something
exampleSentence: The police are looking into the cause of the accident.
exampleTranslation: 警方正在调查事故原因。

definition: To search for information in a reference source; to improve
exampleSentence: If you don't know the word, look it up in the dictionary.
exampleTranslation: 如果你不认识这个词，就在字典里查一下。

definition: To manage to see or understand something with difficulty
exampleSentence: The handwriting was so messy I couldn't make out what it said.
exampleTranslation: 字迹太潦草了，我看不清写的是什么。

definition: To invent a story or excuse; to become friends again after an argument
exampleSentence: The children made up a story about a dragon living in their garden.
exampleTranslation: 孩子们编了一个关于花园里住着一条龙的故事。

definition: To begin living in a new home
exampleSentence: We're moving in to our new apartment next weekend.
exampleTranslation: 我们下周末搬进新公寓。

definition: To start doing something new; to stop thinking about the past
exampleSentence: It's time to move on and focus on new opportunities.
exampleTranslation: 是时候向前看，关注新的机会了。

definition: To die — used as a polite or gentle way to say someone died
exampleSentence: Her grandmother passed away peacefully in her sleep.
exampleTranslation: 她的祖母在睡梦中安详地去世了。

definition: To lose consciousness; to distribute
exampleSentence: He passed out from the extreme heat during the marathon.
exampleTranslation: 他在马拉松比赛中因极度高温而昏倒了。

definition: To return money that you owe to someone
exampleSentence: I'll pay you back as soon as I get my salary.
exampleTranslation: 我一拿到工资就还你钱。

definition: To finish paying a debt; to have a successful result
exampleSentence: All her hard work finally paid off when she got the promotion.
exampleTranslation: 当她升职时，所有的努力终于得到了回报。

definition: To choose or select from a group
exampleSentence: She picked out a beautiful dress for the party.
exampleTranslation: 她为派对挑了一条漂亮的裙子。

definition: To lift something; to collect someone; to learn something informally
exampleSentence: I'll pick you up from the airport at 5 PM.
exampleTranslation: 我下午五点去机场接你。

definition: To draw attention to something; to mention
exampleSentence: She pointed out several errors in the report.
exampleTranslation: 她指出了报告中的几个错误。

definition: To succeed in doing something difficult
exampleSentence: Despite all the challenges, they managed to pull off the event.
exampleTranslation: 尽管面临诸多挑战，他们还是成功举办了活动。

definition: To move a vehicle to the side of the road and stop
exampleSentence: The police officer asked the driver to pull over.
exampleTranslation: 警察让司机靠边停车。

definition: To place something in its proper storage location
exampleSentence: Please put away your toys before dinner.
exampleTranslation: 晚饭前请把玩具收好。

definition: To postpone; to delay doing something
exampleSentence: Stop putting off your homework and start working on it now.
exampleTranslation: 别再拖延作业了，现在就开始做。

definition: To place clothing on your body; to switch on a device
exampleSentence: Put on your jacket — it's cold outside.
exampleTranslation: 穿上你的外套——外面很冷。

definition: To tolerate or endure something unpleasant
exampleSentence: I can't put up with this noise anymore — it's driving me crazy.
exampleTranslation: 我再也受不了这个噪音了——快把我逼疯了。

definition: To meet someone unexpectedly; to encounter a problem
exampleSentence: I ran into my old teacher at the supermarket yesterday.
exampleTranslation: 我昨天在超市偶遇了我的老师。

definition: To use all of something so that there is none left
exampleSentence: We've run out of milk — could you pick some up?
exampleTranslation: 我们的牛奶用完了——你能买一些回来吗？

definition: To hit someone or something with a vehicle; to exceed a time limit
exampleSentence: The meeting ran over by thirty minutes.
exampleTranslation: 会议超时了三十分钟。

definition: To delay the progress of something
exampleSentence: The bad weather set back the construction by two weeks.
exampleTranslation: 恶劣天气使施工推迟了两周。

definition: To begin a journey; to cause something to start
exampleSentence: We set off early in the morning to avoid traffic.
exampleTranslation: 我们一大早就出发以避开交通拥堵。

definition: To establish or arrange something; to assemble equipment
exampleSentence: They set up a new branch office in Shanghai.
exampleTranslation: 他们在上海设立了一个新的分公司。

definition: To display abilities or possessions to impress others
exampleSentence: He's always showing off his expensive watch to everyone.
exampleTranslation: 他总是向所有人炫耀他的名贵手表。

definition: To arrive or appear, often unexpectedly
exampleSentence: He didn't show up for the interview, which was very unprofessional.
exampleTranslation: 他面试没来，这非常不专业。

definition: To stop operating; to close a business permanently
exampleSentence: The factory shut down due to financial problems.
exampleTranslation: 工厂因财务问题而关闭了。

definition: To organize or resolve a problem
exampleSentence: We need to sort out the schedule before the event starts.
exampleTranslation: 我们需要在活动开始前把日程安排好。

definition: To represent or be an abbreviation for something
exampleSentence: UNESCO stands for the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization.
exampleTranslation: UNESCO代表联合国教育、科学及文化组织。

definition: To be noticeably different or better than others
exampleSentence: Her bright red coat made her stand out in the crowd.
exampleTranslation: 她亮红色的外套让她在人群中格外显眼。

definition: To rise to a standing position; to fail to meet someone as planned
exampleSentence: He stood me up — I waited for an hour at the restaurant.
exampleTranslation: 他放了我鸽子——我在餐厅等了一个小时。

definition: To leave the ground and begin to fly; to remove clothing
exampleSentence: The plane took off on time despite the cloudy weather.
exampleTranslation: 尽管天气多云，飞机还是准时起飞了。

definition: To accept a responsibility or challenge; to hire
exampleSentence: She decided to take on the challenging project.
exampleTranslation: 她决定承担这个有挑战性的项目。

definition: To gain control of something; to assume responsibility from someone
exampleSentence: A larger company took over the small startup last year.
exampleTranslation: 一家大公司去年收购了这家小型创业公司。

definition: To start doing a new activity or hobby; to occupy space or time
exampleSentence: She took up painting as a hobby after retiring.
exampleTranslation: 她退休后开始以画画为爱好。

definition: To discard or dispose of something
exampleSentence: Don't throw away those boxes — we can recycle them.
exampleTranslation: 别扔掉那些纸箱——我们可以回收它们。

definition: To vomit
exampleSentence: The rough sea made several passengers throw up.
exampleTranslation: 汹涌的海浪让好几个乘客呕吐了。

definition: To refuse or reject an offer; to reduce the volume or level
exampleSentence: She turned down the job offer because the salary was too low.
exampleTranslation: 她拒绝了那份工作，因为薪水太低了。

definition: To stop a device or machine from working
exampleSentence: Please turn off the lights when you leave the room.
exampleTranslation: 离开房间时请关灯。

definition: To start a device or machine
exampleSentence: Could you turn on the air conditioner? It's really hot in here.
exampleTranslation: 你能打开空调吗？这里真的很热。

definition: To happen in a particular way; to have a particular result
exampleSentence: The party turned out to be much more fun than I expected.
exampleTranslation: 派对结果比我预期的有趣得多。

definition: To arrive or appear; to increase the volume or level
exampleSentence: He turned up at the party without being invited.
exampleTranslation: 他没被邀请就出现在了派对上。

definition: To exercise; to find a solution; to develop in a successful way
exampleSentence: I work out at the gym three times a week.
exampleTranslation: 我每周在健身房锻炼三次。
